existing_claim: I attacked the Decision for scope creep — smuggled policy commitments riding along as principle. §2 declares it fixes invariant/accountability/control-type only, and §2.6 explicitly holds the remedy boundary, thresholds, cadence, retention, reviewer roles, and multi-condition semantics out and routes them to the feedback-loop governance design. The scoping is consistent across §2.2 (control-type vs mechanics), §2.6, §5.3 (control-types not calibrations), and §6 check 5 (boundary check). The record decides what it claims to decide and no more; the attack held.
incoming_claim: I attacked the Decision for scope creep — commitments riding along as principle that are actually policy the forthcoming governance design should own. The attack failed: §2 declares the record fixes only invariant, accountability, and control-type; §2.6 explicitly holds out the remedy boundary (supersede vs retract), thresholds, cadence, retention windows, reviewer-role assignments, and multi-condition composition and routes them downstream; §5.3 restates that the record commits control-types not calibrations; and §6 check 5 (boundary check) makes the exclusion enforceable. The scoping is consistent across all four sites and the record decides no more than it claims.
